topic: sparkling wine lees autolysis and aromatic complexity

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A traditional-method sparkling wine remains on its secondary-fermentation lees for an extended period.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Dead yeast cells undergo autolysis and release amino acids, peptides, mannoproteins, and other compounds that develop with time.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine develops autolytic complexity with aromas such as bread, biscuit, toast, brioche, and pastry.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The wine develops autolytic complexity with aromas such as bread, biscuit, toast, brioche, and pastry.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
